meaning: n.娑備互鐏版偿,鐭宠啅宸ヨ壓
examples: He thought of plastering the station with posters. Then a brainstorm: the Internet.
They would rather see the firm focusing on managing their money than plastering its name on billboards.
Some fashion houses squander their hard fought cool rep by plastering a company logo on everything that moves.
Industry undertook a multimillion-dollar campaign, flying banners over the city and plastering ads over the subways.
Nearly all job seekers are plastering job boards with resumes and praying they receive responses, he says.
